persons.arguments_against: The strongest case against: this is a single-initial biography ('ADAMSON, E') with 22 same-surname officials in the staff lists, so the namesake trap applies, and two other biographies (adamson_alfred-edward_b1871, adamson_st-john-ernest_b1867) are gate-compatible with this stint. The deterministic pass also failed on colony, tenure overlap, and position similarity, suggesting no placed agreement. Why this fails: the deterministic FAILs are an artifact of the timeline compiler mis-inheriting 'Malaya' — the verbatim printed entry explicitly moves the career to Singapore ('senr. exec. engrn., P.W.D., S'pore, 1955') and then states 'D.D.P.W., 1958-60', which expands exactly to the stint's 'Deputy Director of Public Works' in Singapore, listed 1959-1960. Deputy Director of Public Works is a unique senior post in a small colony, not a generic junior title, so a second E. Adamson holding it in the same years is not a realistic counter-hypothesis. The two alternative biographies were born in 1871 and 1867, making them 88 and 92 in 1959 — chronologically implausible for a serving Deputy Director, whereas this biography's subject (b. 1921, P.W.D. engineer in Malaya/Singapore since 1946) is age 38 with a career shape leading directly into the post. Position + place + exact years + initial agree independently.
persons.would_overturn: Evidence of a second E. Adamson in the Singapore Public Works Department in the late 1950s (e.g., a staff-list entry showing two E. Adamsons concurrently, or a forename for the stint that contradicts this biography); or evidence that 'D.D.P.W.' in this biography denotes a post in the Federation of Malaya rather than Singapore, with a different person holding the Singapore Deputy Directorship in 1959-60.

notes: The deterministic colony/tenure/position FAILs are all downstream of one compiler error: the timeline inherited 'Malaya' for the 1955-1960 clauses, but the verbatim text reads 'P.W.D., S'pore, 1955', placing the senior career in Singapore. With that corrected, the biography's 'D.D.P.W., 1958-60' matches the stint's Deputy Director of Public Works, Singapore, 1959-1960 on position abbreviation, place, and years. Edition co-occurrence also holds in fact (bio appears in editions 1959 and 1960, the stint's editions) despite the mechanical FAIL. The two outside gate-compatible biographies (b. 1871, b. 1867) are ruled out on age.
